_comment: Curated SSDF practice -> FedRAMP 20x KSI forward map. NIST publishes NO SSDF<->KSI mapping; this relationship is semantic and is the operator's curation (cf. T.T1 risk T.T1-6 / cross-cutting T-X7). Each pair carries a confidence (high|medium|low) and a rationale grounded in shared NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 controls and/or the CISA Self-Attestation Common Form Section IV linkage. Reviewed at PR time. Every ksi_id MUST exist in core/ksi-map.ts; scripts/extract-ssdf-practices.mjs fails with ERR_SSDF_KSI_UNKNOWN otherwise. Practices with no clear KSI linkage carry no entry (informational gap surfaced by T.T5).

PO.1.rationale: PO.1.3 communicates security requirements to third parties supplying commercial components; both PO.1 and KSI-SCR-MON (Monitoring Supply Chain Risk) trace to SP 800-53 SR-3 (supply chain risk management plan).

PO.3.rationale: PO.3.3 configures toolchains to generate logged artifacts of their operation; KSI-MLA-LET (Logging Event Types) governs which event types the platform logs.

PO.5.rationale: PO.5.1 (separate and protect each development/build environment) is the SSDF basis for CISA Common Form Section IV(1)(c), which requires multi-factor authentication across environments relevant to developing and building software; KSI-IAM-MFA (Enforcing Phishing-Resistant MFA) is the direct FedRAMP control.

Common Form Section IV(1)(b) requires logging, monitoring, and auditing of trust relationships used for authorization and access to development/build environments; KSI-MLA-ALA (Authorizing Log Access) governs who may access those logs.

PS.1.rationale: PS.1.1 stores all forms of code 'based on the principle of least privilege so that only authorized personnel, tools, services, etc. have access'; KSI-IAM-ELP (Ensuring Least Privilege) is the direct FedRAMP control.

PS.2.rationale: PS.2.1 makes software integrity verification information available to acquirers; KSI-SVC-VRI (Validating Resource Integrity) is the runtime analog of release-integrity verification.

PW.4.rationale: PW.4 acquires and verifies third-party software components; both PW.4 and KSI-SCR-MON (Monitoring Supply Chain Risk) trace to SP 800-53 SR-3/SR-4 (supply chain risk management plan and provenance).
PW.4.4 verifies acquired commercial/open-source components meet requirements; KSI-SCR-MIT (Mitigating Supply Chain Risk) is the FedRAMP control for acting on identified supplier risk.

PW.7.rationale: PW.7 reviews/analyzes human-readable code to find vulnerabilities (SP 800-53 SA-11); KSI-CMT-VTD (Validating Throughout Deployment) requires equivalent validation gates throughout the deployment pipeline.

PW.8.rationale: PW.8 tests executable code to find vulnerabilities (SP 800-53 SA-11); KSI-CMT-VTD (Validating Throughout Deployment) requires equivalent validation gates throughout the deployment pipeline.

PW.9.rationale: PW.9 configures software to have secure settings by default; KSI-MLA-EVC (Evaluating Configurations) continuously evaluates that deployed configurations remain secure.

RV.1.rationale: RV.1.1 gathers vulnerability information including for third-party components (SP 800-53 SR-3/SR-4); KSI-SCR-MON (Monitoring Supply Chain Risk) is the ongoing supplier-risk monitoring control.

RV.1.3 requires a vulnerability disclosure program; KSI-INR-RIR (Reviewing Incident Response Procedures) overlaps where disclosure intake feeds incident response. Confidence low: the linkage is partial and surfaced for operator review.

RV.2.rationale: RV.2.2 plans and implements risk responses for vulnerabilities (SP 800-53 SA-11); KSI-CMT-RMV (Redeploying vs Modifying) is the FedRAMP control for remediating by redeploying fixed artifacts rather than mutating running ones.

RV.3.rationale: RV.3.4 reviews and updates the SDLC process after root-cause analysis; KSI-CMT-RVP (Reviewing Change Procedures) overlaps on periodic process review. Confidence low: surfaced for operator review.
RV.3.1/RV.3.2 analyze root causes over time; KSI-INR-RPI (Reviewing Past Incidents) is the incident-side analog of recurring root-cause analysis. Confidence low: surfaced for operator review.
